chapter_title.en: The Prostration of Gratitude (sujood Al-shukr) and What to Say in it

parts.text: 976 - وَقَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «إِنَّ اَلْعَبْدَ إِذَا سَجَدَ فَقَالَ

text: يَا رَبِّ يَا رَبِّ حَتَّى يَنْقَطِعَ نَفَسُهُ قَالَ لَهُ اَلرَّبُّ تَبَارَكَ وتَعَالَى «لَبَّيْكَ مَا حَاجَتُكَ ».

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.976 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"When a servant prostrates and says, 'O’ Lord (azj), O’ Lord (azj),' until their breath runs out, the Lord (azj), Blessed and Exalted, responds,
'Here I am; what is your need?'".
